title: Ports, Protocols, Services, and Traffic Types · Packet Forensics
summary: Build and diagnose a service-access matrix from client, firewall, resolver, server, and capture evidence. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a verified protocol and service matrix and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use N10-009 standard ports for file transfer, remote administration, mail, DNS, DHCP, web, time, monitoring, directory, database, desktop, and voice services.
Compare secure and insecure service alternatives such as SSH or Telnet, HTTPS or HTTP, LDAPS or LDAP, and SFTP or FTP.
Explain how DNS, DHCP, NTP, PTP, and NTS dependencies affect authentication, addressing, logging, and service reachability.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.
